creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual
videos.summary: Matt from AI Engineer presents guidance on building effective agent skills, framed as a comprehensive manual for agent development. This appears to be the third contribution from the same creator on the topic.

videos.title: Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize
videos.summary: RL Nabors from Arize argues that most applications can use small local models instead of frontier models, reducing latency, energy, and cost. He presents a framework using capability evals, golden datasets, and LLM-as-judge to measure when local models are sufficient and shares production examples including agentic systems, moderation classifiers, and summarization features.

videos.title: The Future Is Domain-Specific Agents - Justin Schroeder, StandardAgents
videos.summary: Justin Schroeder argues that domain-specific agents composed together deliver superior performance, reliability, and cost efficiency compared to traditional skill-based approaches. He claims this architecture enables smaller models to handle real work and shifts AI from internal copilots to customer-facing products.

videos.title: You Can't Prompt the Room: The Last Skill AI Won't Replace - Balázs Horváth, VisualLabs
videos.summary: Balázs Horváth argues that as AI handles code generation, the bottleneck shifts to human-centered work: stakeholder alignment, requirements elicitation, and value validation. He presents practical frameworks (story mapping, 4-question value framework, VAD thinking path) to bridge this gap, illustrated by VisualLabs' experience where only 4 of 21 hackathon agent ideas reached production.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin breaks down Google's 50-page whitepaper on agentic engineering for software development, arguing that the AI model is only 10% of success while the surrounding harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) comprises the other 90%. The video positions agentic engineering as the future direction, contrasting it with "vibe coding" and emphasizing the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles for developers.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is a decentralized memory layer that enables AI agents from different providers to share context across tools. It encrypts memories before storage, allows user ownership of keys, and supports fine-grained access control across multiple AI providers like Claude, Codex, and Pi.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Kimi K2.7 Code is an open-source, coding-focused agentic model developed by Moonshot AI. The reviewer notes it offers strong performance for its cost and parameter scale.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses how AI engineers use self-prompting loops instead of manual prompting, where an orchestrator agent spins up worker agents to run tasks in parallel. He demonstrates this with a TypeScript dashboard for Pi loops that stores runs in a database and balances cost with reliability.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses a secret project called Crabbox with a Codebase harness, available via GitHub repository for setup. The project is part of the AI Builder Club community and Superdesign platform offering.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: Loop Engineer is a framework or tool for building AI agents. The content provides setup guidance, a GitHub template, and workshop materials for users to get started building their first AI agent.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems using AI loops. Covers use cases like autonomous ads optimization and SEO, and discusses tools like Loopany that enable this through memory and tooling.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses emerging autonomous coding tools and features. The video covers the /goal feature for setting coding objectives, goal-buddy functionality, and mission exploration capabilities for executing longer-horizon tasks autonomously.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: GPT 5.6 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code discusses OpenAI's announcement of GPT 5.6, which faces government regulation as the AI industry matures. The video compares GPT 5.6 to Anthropic's Mythos 5 and Fable 5 models, both paused pending regulatory review. OpenAI is consolidating its model lineup to three offerings named Sol, Terra, and Luna.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains the mechanics of generative pretrained transformers, covering self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections. The video provides an overview of how foundational transformer-based models like GPT, Gemini, Opus, Kimi, Nemotron, and Qwen function at a fundamental level.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains Andrej Karpathy's Bigram Language Model, covering tokenization, embeddings, training, loss functions, backpropagation, and optimization. The video serves as foundational material for understanding GPT and transformer-based language models.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 series including Nano, Super, and Ultra models. The architecture combines hybrid mamba transformer design with latent mixture of experts and MTP mechanisms.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
videos.summary: AWS Developers published a system design tutorial for beginners that advocates a seven-step problem-first framework: requirements, traffic estimation, high-level components, API contracts, tech stack, and implementation constraints. The video frames system design interviews around solving the problem before selecting technology.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: AWS Developers video explaining how tool calling transforms AI agents from chatbots that guess into functional systems with real-time data access. Covers tool architecture (schema, description, handler), the loop mechanism where models request tools and frameworks execute them, and the role of system prompts in tool selection.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS is a framework that equips AI coding agents with up-to-date AWS documentation, best practices, and tested procedures. It improves agent success rates by providing access to current knowledge rather than relying on outdated general knowledge of AWS services.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: Claude Code is an agentic coding tool by Anthropic that automates development tasks. AWS Developers explains how to deploy and run Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock, ensuring data residency within AWS infrastructure while enabling cross-region inference, prompt caching, cost tracking, and content governance.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A video commentary examining code generation practices at Google and comparing OpenAI's Codex with Claude Code. The video segments discuss what "vibing code" (likely shorthand for generating or using code generation) means, current practices at Google, and speculative future trends through 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines Malus, a company or tool positioned as disruptive to open source, discussing its implications for open source maintainers and the ecosystem. The video frames the topic as a provocative debate about whether Malus represents an existential threat to open source.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: What you must know before Mythos arrives | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
videos.summary: Damien Lewke argues that AI has drastically reduced the barrier to launching cyberattacks, enabling small teams with just a GPU to execute sophisticated campaigns that previously required elite teams and years of expertise. He outlines the cyber kill chain stages, identifies three behavioral signals that reveal active attackers inside networks, and advocates for an "assume breach" defensive posture as the only realistic security model.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that code alone is insufficient competitive moat; customer context, proprietary data, and operational complexity at scale provide the real advantage. He contends that fears of AI disrupting SaaS companies are overstated and that the best AI products succeed by creating raving fans rather than simply satisfying customers.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan, a Stanford roboticist, argues that while a transformative moment for robotics is coming, the current focus on humanoid robots led by companies like 1X and Unitree is myopic. She contends that designing robots for human interaction and delight, rather than utility alone, will be critical to shaping the billions of robots entering our lives.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her AI performance management platform competes against much larger enterprise software giants. She shares lessons from observing Google's reliance on spreadsheets for strategic planning and explains the strategic advantage of building a full platform from day one rather than shipping single features.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Slash SaaS Costs: Build Your Own AI CRM with Chinese Models! #shorts
videos.summary: Organizations are shifting away from expensive SaaS platforms like CRMs to build proprietary AI-powered alternatives. Chinese AI models are presented as cost-effective alternatives for these system overhauls.

videos.title: AI is KILLING your SaaS! Here's WHY. #shorts
videos.summary: AI agents are evolving rapidly and pose a competitive threat to traditional SaaS businesses. The video discusses how companies need to adapt to survive in a changing AI-driven landscape.

videos.title: Slash AI Costs: Smart Model Choice for Max ROAI! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof advocates for strategic model selection based on task complexity to optimize Return on AI Investment (ROAI) and reduce costs. The core argument is that not all tasks require powerful models, and matching model complexity to actual needs improves efficiency.

videos.title: AI Ecosystems: Claude vs. SAP/Salesforce & Living Artifacts Explained! #shorts
videos.summary: The video explores trade-offs between expensive AI queries and self-updating living artifacts as approaches to dynamic system integration. It examines the cost-effectiveness of different AI ecosystem architectures for enterprise systems.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
